input: nanti dulu aku mau buat roadmap sebentar

parts.text:
I am building a long-term SaaS product as a solo developer.

I need you to create a professional Markdown progress tracking file for my project.

I want a file called:

PROJECT_ROADMAP.md

Important context:

I am building a SaaS business operating system.

Current market:

* UMKM (small businesses in Indonesia)

Future expansion:

* Restaurant businesses
* Hospitality / Villa management

Tech stack:

Frontend:

* React
* Vite

Backend:

* Node.js
* Express.js

Database:

* MongoDB

Infrastructure:

* Docker
* Redis

Architecture:

* Multi-tenant SaaS
* Modular Monolith
* Domain Driven Design (DDD)
* Event Driven Architecture

I want the markdown file to act as my engineering execution tracker.

The file must be structured professionally.

Create the markdown with these sections.

=========================================
SECTION 1 — PROJECT OVERVIEW
============================

Include:

* Project name
* Vision
* Target market
* Current development phase
* Current architecture decisions
* Long term roadmap

Example:

Current Phase:
MVP Development

Architecture:
Modular Monolith + Multi Tenant

=========================================
SECTION 2 — MASTER ROADMAP
==========================

Create major phases.

Example:

PHASE A — Foundation Setup

PHASE B — Core Backend Development

PHASE C — Core Frontend Development

PHASE D — POS Engine

PHASE E — Payment System

PHASE F — Reporting

PHASE G — Testing

PHASE H — MVP Deployment

Each phase should have completion percentage.

Example:

[ ] Not Started
[~] In Progress
[x] Completed

=========================================
SECTION 3 — WEEKLY DEVELOPMENT CHECKLIST
========================================

Example:

WEEK 1

[ ] Monorepo Setup
[ ] Docker Setup
[ ] Backend Folder Structure
[ ] Frontend Folder Structure

WEEK 2

[ ] Authentication
[ ] JWT
[ ] Tenant System
[ ] RBAC

WEEK 3

[ ] Product Catalog
[ ] Categories
[ ] SKU System

WEEK 4

[ ] Inventory
[ ] Stock Movement
[ ] Stock Adjustment

Continue until MVP completion.

=========================================
SECTION 4 — MVP FEATURE CHECKLIST
=================================

Track MVP progress.

Authentication

[ ]

Tenant Management

[ ]

Product Management

[ ]

Inventory Management

[ ]

POS Cart

[ ]

Checkout Process

[ ]

Cash Payment

[ ]

Receipt Printing

[ ]

Basic Reporting

[ ]

Bug Fixing

[ ]

MVP Ready

[ ]

=========================================
SECTION 5 — FUTURE FEATURES (DO NOT BUILD YET)
==============================================

Create delayed features list.

Example:

[ ] Restaurant Module

[ ] Kitchen Printer

[ ] Table Management

[ ] Villa Module

[ ] Reservation System

[ ] Mobile App via Capacitor

[ ] Desktop App via Electron

[ ] Offline Sync

[ ] Bluetooth Printer

[ ] AI Automation

[ ] Plugin Runtime

These features must be clearly marked:

NOT MVP

=========================================
SECTION 6 — CURRENT ARCHITECTURE STATUS
=======================================

Track architecture progress.

System Architecture

[x]

DDD Design

[x]

Database Design

[x]

API Design

[x]

Authentication Design

[x]

RBAC Design

[x]

Event Bus Design

[x]

Infrastructure Design

[x]

Transaction Engine Design

[x]

Offline Sync Design

[ ]

Printer Architecture

[ ]

Plugin Architecture

[ ]

=========================================
SECTION 7 — CURRENT BLOCKERS
============================

Track technical blockers.

Example:

Current blocker:

Printer strategy not decided.

Current blocker:

Need tenant middleware architecture.

Need sections:

Problem

Possible solutions

Priority

Deadline

Status

=========================================
SECTION 8 — DAILY ENGINEERING LOG
=================================

Example:

DATE: 2026-06-30

Today I worked on:

* Docker setup
* Monorepo structure
* API base structure

Problems found:

* Redis connection issue

Next task:

* Setup authentication module

Need daily log template.

=========================================
SECTION 9 — TECHNICAL DEBT TRACKER
==================================

Track bad code or shortcuts.

Example:

Need refactor auth middleware

Need improve inventory service

Need optimize MongoDB indexes

Need better error handling

=========================================
SECTION 10 — PERSONAL RULES
===========================

Add rules for solo developer discipline.

Example:

Rule 1

Do not build non-MVP features.

Rule 2

Do not refactor without reason.

Rule 3

Ship working features first.

Rule 4

Do not add restaurant module yet.

Rule 5

Avoid overengineering.

Rule 6

Validate with real users early.

=========================================
SECTION 11 — SUCCESS METRICS
============================

Track business progress.

Example:

First working local MVP

[ ]

First deployed server

[ ]

First pilot customer

[ ]

First active tenant

[ ]

First paying customer

[ ]

100 transactions successfully processed

[ ]

=========================================
IMPORTANT
=========

Output ONLY Markdown.

Make it clean and beautiful.

Make it look like a real startup engineering tracker.

It should be practical for daily use.

This file will be stored inside my project repository and updated every day.

input: oke sekarang posisi kita dimana kalo berdasarkan progess ini
sebentar bantu aku bikin ini dulu [Pasted ~338 lines]

parts.text:
I am building a serious long-term SaaS product as a solo developer.

I want to organize my engineering work professionally.

I need you to generate a complete documentation system for my local repository.

Create a documentation folder structure.

Output multiple markdown files.

Folder structure:

docs/

PROJECT_ROADMAP.md
DAILY_LOG.md
ARCHITECTURE.md
BACKLOG.md
BUG_TRACKER.md
DECISIONS.md

Project context:

I am building a SaaS business operating system.

Current target market:

* UMKM in Indonesia

Future expansion:

* Restaurant businesses
* Hospitality / Villa management

Tech stack:

Frontend:

* React
* Vite

Backend:

* Node.js
* Express.js

Database:

* MongoDB

Infrastructure:

* Docker
* Redis

Architecture decisions:

* Multi-tenant SaaS
* Domain Driven Design (DDD)
* Modular Monolith
* Event Driven Architecture

I want you to generate ALL markdown files.

=========================================
FILE 1 — PROJECT_ROADMAP.md
===========================

Purpose:

Master roadmap for the entire project.

Need sections:

* Project vision
* Development phases
* Weekly progress checklist
* MVP checklist
* Future features
* Development status percentage
* Milestones

Use checkbox format.

Example:

[ ] Not Started
[~] In Progress
[x] Completed

=========================================
FILE 2 — DAILY_LOG.md
=====================

Purpose:

Daily engineering journal.

Need sections:

For every day:

Date

Today I worked on:

Problems encountered:

What I completed:

What I learned:

Tomorrow priority:

Current mood / productivity score:

Example template:

DATE: 2026-06-30

Worked on:

* Docker setup
* API folder structure

Problems:

* Mongo connection issue

Completed:

* Monorepo setup

Tomorrow:

* Authentication module

Make reusable daily template.

=========================================
FILE 3 — ARCHITECTURE.md
========================

Purpose:

Architecture documentation.

Need sections:

* High level architecture
* Folder structure
* Domain Driven Design domains
* Database architecture
* Event driven architecture
* Multi tenant strategy
* API structure
* Authentication flow
* Infrastructure architecture

Add architecture diagrams in markdown code block.

Example:

Frontend

↓

API Gateway

↓

Modules

* Identity
* Tenant
* Orders
* Payments
* Inventory

↓

MongoDB

=========================================
FILE 4 — BACKLOG.md
===================

Purpose:

Store ideas that should NOT be built now.

Need sections:

MVP features

Post-MVP features

Long term features

Future modules

Possible backlog:

Restaurant Module

Villa Module

Kitchen Printer

Bluetooth Printer

Offline Sync

Mobile App via Capacitor

Desktop App via Electron

AI Automation

Analytics Dashboard

Public API

Marketplace Integration

Plugin System

Mark priorities:

LOW

MEDIUM

HIGH

DO NOT BUILD NOW

=========================================
FILE 5 — BUG_TRACKER.md
=======================

Purpose:

Track technical issues.

Need format:

BUG ID

Description

Severity

Possible Cause

Status

Assigned Date

Fixed Date

Example:

BUG-001

Problem:
JWT refresh token not rotating correctly

Severity:
HIGH

Status:
OPEN

Need reusable bug template.

=========================================
FILE 6 — DECISIONS.md
=====================

Purpose:

Track technical decisions.

I want to remember WHY I chose something.

Need format:

Decision ID

Date

Problem

Possible Options

Chosen Option

Reasoning

Future Revisit Date

Example:

DECISION-001

Problem:
Need database strategy

Options:
Database per tenant
Shared database

Chosen:
Shared database

Reason:
Cheaper for MVP

Need reusable decision template.

=========================================
GENERAL REQUIREMENTS
====================

Output all markdown files.

Make everything clean.

Use startup engineering style.

This documentation system will be used daily.

Optimize for solo developer workflow.

Avoid unnecessary complexity.

Make it feel like internal documentation at a serious SaaS startup.

I have finished designing the architecture of my SaaS project.

Architecture work is DONE.

I do NOT want to redesign architecture anymore.

I am now entering BUILD MODE.

Your role:

Act as a senior software engineer and technical lead helping me IMPLEMENT the MVP step by step.

Important:

Do NOT redesign architecture unless absolutely necessary.

Do NOT suggest unnecessary enterprise complexity.

Do NOT introduce microservices.

Do NOT introduce overengineering.

Focus on execution.

Project context:

I am building a long-term SaaS business operating system.

Current target market:

Small businesses (UMKM) in Indonesia.

Future expansion:

Restaurant businesses.

Hospitality / Villa management.

Current architecture decisions:

* Multi-tenant SaaS architecture
* Modular Monolith architecture
* Domain Driven Design (DDD)
* Event Driven internal architecture

Tech stack:

Frontend:

* React
* Vite

Backend:

* Node.js
* Express.js

Database:

* MongoDB

Infrastructure:

* Docker
* Redis

Current development phase:

PHASE B — MVP CORE DEVELOPMENT

MVP goal:

A tenant can:

Login

Create products

Manage inventory

Create transactions

Receive payment

Print receipt

See basic sales report

I want to build ONLY MVP.

Do NOT suggest future features.

Do NOT mention restaurant module.

Do NOT mention villa module.

Do NOT mention mobile apps.

Do NOT mention offline sync.

Do NOT mention plugin architecture.

Do NOT mention microservices.

Ignore future architecture.

====================================================
YOUR JOB
========

Help me build the MVP step by step.

We will build modules ONE BY ONE.

Only focus on the current module.

Do NOT jump ahead.

For each module, you must help me with:

1. Folder structure

2. Domain structure

3. Database schema

4. API endpoints

5. Service layer

6. Repository layer

7. Validation layer

8. Business rules

9. Edge cases

10. Testing strategy

====================================================
BUILD ORDER
===========

We MUST follow this exact order.

STEP 1

Identity Module

Need:

* Login
* Logout
* JWT authentication
* Refresh token
* Protected routes

Collections:

* users
* roles
* sessions

Roles:

* owner
* manager
* cashier

Do NOT build:

* forgot password
* email verification
* oauth login

---

STEP 2

Tenant Module

Need:

* Create tenant
* Tenant settings
* Tenant isolation
* Owner assignment

Every collection must support:

tenantId

---

STEP 3

Catalog Module

Need:

* Create product
* Update product
* Delete product
* Categories
* SKU
* Price
* Barcode field

Collections:

* products
* categories

Do NOT build:

* variants
* bundles
* suppliers

---

STEP 4

Inventory Module

Need:

* Stock in
* Stock out
* Stock adjustment
* Stock movement history

Collections:

* inventory_items
* stock_movements

Do NOT build:

* warehouses
* suppliers
* purchase orders

---

STEP 5

Orders Module

Need:

* Cart
* Add item
* Remove item
* Calculate total
* Tax
* Discount
* Checkout

Order states:

* draft
* pending
* paid
* cancelled

Collections:

* orders
* order_items

Do NOT build:

* split bill
* dine in
* kitchen orders

---

STEP 6

Payments Module

Need:

* Cash payment only

Collections:

* payments

Do NOT build:

* QRIS
* Midtrans integration
* refunds

---

STEP 7

Receipt Module

Need:

* HTML receipt template
* Browser print
* Invoice generation

Do NOT build:

* Bluetooth printer
* ESC/POS printer

---

STEP 8

Reporting Module

Need:

* Today sales
* Revenue summary
* Transaction count
* Top selling products

Do NOT build:

* Analytics dashboard
* Forecasting
* AI reports

====================================================
WORKING STYLE
=============

I am building solo.

Help me avoid complexity.

Always prioritize:

Simple

Maintainable

Fast execution

Low technical debt

Production-safe enough

====================================================
VERY IMPORTANT
==============

Never jump ahead.

Never redesign architecture.

Never suggest future features.

Never overengineer.

Always focus on current module only.

When I say:

START STEP 1

You only help me build Identity Module.

When I say:

START STEP 2

You only help me build Tenant Module.

And continue sequentially.

We are now in BUILD MODE.

parts.text:
I am building a serious long-term SaaS application as a solo developer.

My backend architecture is already implemented.

I now need a professional testing documentation file for my repository.

I want you to generate a markdown file named:

TESTING_STRATEGY.md

Purpose:

This file will act as the central testing documentation for my entire backend architecture.

This file will live inside:

docs/TESTING_STRATEGY.md

Project context:

I am building a SaaS business operating system.

Current market:

* UMKM in Indonesia

Future expansion:

* Restaurant businesses
* Hospitality / Villa management

Backend stack:

* Node.js
* Express.js

Database:

* MongoDB

Infrastructure:

* Docker
* Redis

Architecture decisions:

* Multi-tenant SaaS
* Modular Monolith
* Domain Driven Design (DDD)
* Repository Pattern
* Event Driven Architecture

Testing stack:

* Vitest
* Bruno for API testing

Current implemented modules:

core/identity/      ✅
core/tenant/        ✅
core/catalog/       ✅
core/inventory/     ✅
core/ordering/      ⏳ partial
core/payment/       ⏳ partial
core/pos/           ⏳ partial

I want a PROFESSIONAL testing strategy document.

Output ONLY markdown.

====================================================
SECTION 1 — TESTING PHILOSOPHY
==============================

Explain overall testing philosophy.

Principles:

* Business logic must be tested first
* Frontend testing postponed
* Automated testing preferred
* Testing should happen continuously
* Prevent regressions before adding features

Add development rule:

Never add new feature without adding tests.

====================================================
SECTION 2 — TESTING LAYERS
==========================

Create layered testing strategy.

Layer 1

Domain Testing

Test:

* Entities
* Value Objects
* Aggregate Roots
* Business rules

Layer 2

Service Testing

Test:

* Application services
* Business workflows
* Use cases

Layer 3

Repository Testing

Test:

* Database persistence
* Queries
* Tenant filtering
* MongoDB indexes

Layer 4

API Testing

Test:

* Endpoints
* Middleware
* Authentication
* Authorization

Layer 5

Integration Testing

Test:

* Full request flow
* Database writes
* Module communication

Show architecture diagram in markdown.

Example:

Domain

↓

Service

↓

Repository

↓

API

↓

Integration

====================================================
SECTION 3 — TEST FOLDER STRUCTURE
=================================

Design test folder structure.

Example:

apps/api/src/tests/

domain/

services/

repositories/

api/

integration/

fixtures/

factories/

helpers/

mock-data/

Need scalable structure.

====================================================
SECTION 4 — MODULE TEST COVERAGE
================================

Track coverage goals per module.

Identity Module

Target Coverage:

90%

Need tests:

* Login
* Logout
* JWT validation
* Refresh token
* Role validation

Tenant Module

Target Coverage:

90%

Need tests:

* Create tenant
* Tenant isolation
* Tenant settings

Catalog Module

Target Coverage:

85%

Need tests:

* Create product
* Update product
* Delete product
* Category CRUD

Inventory Module

Target Coverage:

85%

Need tests:

* Stock in
* Stock out
* Stock adjustment
* Stock movement history

POS Module

Target Coverage:

95%

Need tests:

* Cart
* Checkout
* Stock validation

Payments Module

Target Coverage:

95%

Need tests:

* Payment validation
* Order status update
* Change calculation

====================================================
SECTION 5 — MULTI TENANT TESTING RULES
======================================

This is the most important section.

Need SaaS isolation tests.

Critical test cases:

Tenant A cannot read Tenant B data.

Tenant A cannot update Tenant B products.

Tenant A cannot update Tenant B inventory.

Tenant A cannot access Tenant B users.

Tenant A cannot see Tenant B orders.

Create mandatory SaaS security checklist.

====================================================
SECTION 6 — API TESTING STRATEGY
================================

Tool:

Bruno

Need API collections.

Collections:

auth/

tenant/

catalog/

inventory/

payments/

orders/

Need environment variables.

Example:

BASE_URL

ACCESS_TOKEN

REFRESH_TOKEN

TENANT_ID

Need reusable auth flow.

====================================================
SECTION 7 — DATABASE TESTING STRATEGY
=====================================

Need separate test database.

Use:

MongoDB test container.

Rules:

Never test against development database.

Need tests for:

Indexes

Writes

Reads

Pagination

Tenant filtering

Unique constraints

Need database reset strategy.

====================================================
SECTION 8 — TEST PRIORITY ORDER
===============================

Create realistic priority.

Priority 1

Identity module

Priority 2

Tenant module

Priority 3

Catalog module

Priority 4

Inventory module

Priority 5

POS module

Priority 6

Payments module

Do NOT test everything immediately.

Prioritize critical flows.

====================================================
SECTION 9 — CRITICAL BUSINESS FLOWS
===================================

Must never break.

Flow 1

Login

↓

Create tenant

↓

Create product

↓

Add stock

Flow 2

Cashier login

↓

Create cart

↓

Checkout

↓

Payment

↓

Receipt generation

Flow 3

Tenant isolation validation

Tenant A cannot access Tenant B data

Create critical regression checklist.

====================================================
SECTION 10 — TEST FAILURE LOG
=============================

Need reusable template.

Example:

TEST ID

Date

Module

Failed test

Reason

Severity

Temporary fix

Permanent fix

Status

Need markdown template.

====================================================
SECTION 11 — WEEKLY TESTING ROADMAP
===================================

Week 1

Setup testing infrastructure

Install Vitest

Configure test scripts

Create fixtures

Week 2

Test identity module

Week 3

Test tenant module

Week 4

Test catalog module

Week 5

Test inventory module

Week 6

Test POS module

Week 7

Test payment module

Week 8

Regression testing

====================================================
SECTION 12 — ENGINEERING RULES
==============================

Create discipline rules.

Rule 1

Never merge untested code.

Rule 2

Never deploy without API tests.

Rule 3

Never add new endpoint without tests.

Rule 4

Always test tenant isolation.

Rule 5

Business logic testing is mandatory.

Rule 6

Prevent regressions before adding features.

====================================================
IMPORTANT
=========

Output ONLY markdown.

Make it professional.

Make it look like internal engineering documentation from a serious SaaS startup.

Optimize for solo developer workflow.

Long-term maintainability is critical.
